H1.strongs_def: father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application
H1.kjv_def: chief, (fore-) father(-less), [idiom] patrimony, principal. Compare names in 'Abi-'.
H1.usage: chief(2x), families(2x), desire(1x), patrimony(1x), prince(1x), principal(1x)

H6.strongs_def: properly, to wander away, i.e. lose oneself; by implication to perish (causative, destroy)
H6.kjv_def: break, destroy(-uction), [phrase] not escape, fail, lose, (cause to, make) perish, spend, [idiom] and surely, take, be undone, [idiom] utterly, be void of, have no way to flee.
H6.usage: perish(98x), destroy(62x), lose(10x), fail(2x), surely(2x), utterly(2x), broken(1x), destruction(1x), escape(1x), flee(1x), spendeth(1x), take(1x), undone(1x), void(1x)

H7.strongs_def: {properly, to wander away, i.e. lose oneself; by implication to perish (causative, destroy)}

H9.strongs_def: concrete, something lost; abstract, destruction, i.e. Hades

H10.derivation: the same as H9 (אֲבֵדָה), miswritten for H11 (אֲבַדּוֹן);

H14.strongs_def: to breathe after, i.e. (figuratively) to be acquiescent
H14.kjv_def: consent, rest content will, be willing.

H16.derivation: from H14 (אָבָה) (in the sense of bending toward);

H17.derivation: from H14 (אָבָה) (in the sense of desiring);

H19.derivation: from an unused root (apparently meaning to turn);

H22.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H410 (אֵל); father (i.e. possessor) of God;
H22.strongs_def: Abiel, the name of two Israelites

H23.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H622 (אָסַף); father of gathering (i.e. gatherer);

H24.derivation: from an unused root (meaning to be tender);
H24.strongs_def: green, i.e. a young ear of grain; hence, the name of the month Abib or Nisan
H24.kjv_def: Abib, ear, green ears of corn (not maize).

H25.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H1391 (גִּבְעוֹן); father (i.e. founder) of Gibon;

H26.derivation: or shorter אֲבִיגַל; from H1 (אָב) and H1524 (גִּיל); father (i.e. source) of joy;
H26.strongs_def: Abigail or Abigal, the name of two Israelitesses

H27.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H1777 (דִּין); father of judgment (i.e. judge);

H28.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H3045 (יָדַע); father of knowledge (i.e. knowing);
H28.strongs_def: Abida, a son of Abraham by Keturah

H29.derivation: or prolonged אֲבִיָּהוּ ; from H1 (אָב) and H3050 (יָהּ); father (i.e. worshipper) of Jah;
H29.strongs_def: Abijah, the name of several Israelite men and two Israelitesses

H30.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H1931 (הוּא); father (i.e. worshipper) of Him (i.e. God);

H31.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H1935 (הוֹד); father (i.e. possessor) of renown;
H31.strongs_def: Abihud, the name of two Israelites

H32.derivation: or (more correctly) אֲבִיחַיִל ; from H1 (אָב) and H2428 (חַיִל); father (i.e. possessor) of might;
H32.strongs_def: Abihail or Abichail, the name of three Israelites and two Israelitesses

H33.derivation: from H44 (אֲבִיעֶזֶר) with the article inserted; father of the Ezrite;
H33.strongs_def: an Abiezrite or descendant of Abiezer

H34.derivation: from H14 (אָבָה), in the sense of want (especially in feeling);

H35.strongs_def: provocative of desire; the caper berry (from its stimulative taste)

H36.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H2898 (טוּב); father of goodness (i.e. good);

H37.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H2919 (טַל); father of dew (i.e. fresh);
H37.strongs_def: Abital, a wife of King David

H38.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H3220 (יָם); father of (the) sea (i.e. seaman);
H38.strongs_def: Abijam (or Abijah), a king of Judah

H39.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and an elsewhere unused (probably foreign) word; father of Mael (apparently some Arab tribe);

H40.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); father of (the) king;
H40.strongs_def: Abimelek, the name of two Philistine kings and of two Israelites

H41.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H5068 (נָדַב); father of generosity (i.e. liberal);
H41.strongs_def: Abinadab, the name of four Israelites

H42.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H5278 (נֹעַם); father of pleasantness (i.e. gracious);

H44.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H5829 (עֵזֶר); father of help (i.e. helpful);
H44.strongs_def: Abiezer, the name of two Israelites

H45.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and an unused root of uncertain derivation; probably, father of strength (i.e. valiant);

H47.kjv_def: angel, bull, chiefest, mighty (one), stout(-hearted), strong (one), valiant.
H47.usage: bulls(4x), mighty(3x), stouthearted(2x), valiant(2x), angels(1x), chiefest(1x)

H48.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H7311 (רוּם); father of height (i.e. lofty);
H48.strongs_def: Abiram, the name of two Israelites

H49.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H7686 (שָׁגָה); father of error (i.e. blundering);

H50.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H7771 (שׁוֹעַ); father of plenty (i.e. prosperous);
H50.strongs_def: Abishua, the name of two Israelites

H51.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H7791 (שׁוּר); father of (the) wall (i.e. perhaps mason);

H52.derivation: or (shorter) אַבְשַׁי ; from H1 (אָב) and H7862 (שַׁי); father of a gift (i.e. probably generous);

H53.derivation: or (shortened) אַבְשָׁלוֹם ; from H1 (אָב) and H7965 (שָׁלוֹם); father of peace (i.e. friendly);
H53.strongs_def: Abshalom, a son of David; also (the fuller form) a later Israelite

H54.derivation: contracted from H1 (אָב) and H3498 (יָתַר); father of abundance (i.e. liberal);

H58.derivation: from an unused root (meaning to be grassy);
H58.kjv_def: plain. Compare also the proper names beginning with Abel-.

H59.strongs_def: Abel, the name of two places in Palestine.

H61.derivation: apparently from H56 (אָבַל) through the idea of negation;

H62.derivation: from H58 (אָבֵל) and H1004 (בַּיִת) and H4601 (מַעֲכָה); meadow of Beth-Maakah;
H62.strongs_def: Abel of Beth-maakah, a place in Palestine

H63.derivation: from H58 (אָבֵל) and the plural of H7848 (שִׁטָּה), with the article inserted; meadow of the acacias;
H63.strongs_def: Abel hash-Shittim, a place in Palestine

H64.derivation: from H58 (אָבֵל) and the plural of H3754 (כֶּרֶם); meadow of vineyards;

H65.derivation: from H58 (אָבֵל) and H4246 (מְחֹלָה)xlit mᵉchôwlâh corrected to mᵉchôlâh; meadow of dancing;

H66.derivation: from H58 (אָבֵל) and H4325 (מַיִם); meadow of water;

H67.derivation: from H58 (אָבֵל) and H4714 (מִצְרַיִם); meadow of Egypt;

H68.derivation: from the root of H1129 (בָּנָה) through the meaning to build;
H68.kjv_def: [phrase] carbuncle, [phrase] mason, [phrase] plummet, (chalk-, hail-, head-, sling-) stone(-ny), (divers) weight(-s).

H70.strongs_def: a pair of stones (only dual); a potter's wheel or a midwife's stool (consisting alike of two horizontal disks with a support between)

H71.derivation: perhaps feminine of H68 (אֶבֶן); stony;  Compare H549 (אֲמָנָה).

H72.derivation: from H68 (אֶבֶן) and H5828 (עֵזֶר) with the article inserted; stone of the help;

H74.derivation: or (fully) אֲבִינֵר ; from H1 (אָב) and H5216 (נִיר); father of light (i.e. enlightening);

H76.derivation: (by reduplication) from an unused root (meaning to belch forth);

H77.derivation: from an unused root probably meaning to gleam; conspicuous;

H78.derivation: from the same as H76 (אֲבַעְבֻּעָה); splendid;

H79.derivation: a primitive root, probably to float away (as vapor), but used only as denominative from H80 (אָבָק);

H85.derivation: contracted from H1 (אָב) and an unused root (probably meaning to be populous); father of a multitude;
H85.strongs_def: Abraham, the later name of Abram

H87.strongs_def: Abram, the original name of Abraham

H88.strongs_def: Oboth, a place in the Desert

H90.derivation: or אֲגָג; of uncertain derivation (compare H89 (אָגֵא)); flame;
H90.strongs_def: Agag, a title of Amalekitish kings

H91.strongs_def: an Agagite or descendent (subject) of Agag

H92.derivation: feminine passive participle of an unused root (meaning to bind);
H92.strongs_def: a band, bundle, knot, or arch

H94.derivation: passive participle of H103 (אָגַר); gathered (i.e. received among the sages);
H94.strongs_def: Agur, a fanciful name for Solomon

H95.strongs_def: properly, something gathered, i.e. perhaps a grain or berry; used only of a small (silver) coin

H96.derivation: from an unused root (meaning to flow down or together as drops);

H97.derivation: dual of H96 (אֶגֶל).; a double pond;

H98.derivation: from an unused root (meaning to collect as water);
H98.strongs_def: a marsh; hence a rush (as growing in swamps); hence a stockade of reeds

H99.derivation: probably from the same as H98 (אֲגַם) (in the sense of stagnant water);
